title: Pause-until-resolved: a three-layer deterministic circuit breaker so the loop STOPS dispatching when blocked instead of burning waves on zero progress (operator directive 2026-07-16)
summary: Operator: cycles 853+854 burned two full pipelines false-FAILing the SAME task with the SAME signature; the loop had every signal (failedApproaches records existed — the 2026-07-16 failure-learning fix works; cycle-854's retro even wrote 'Re-landing a third time will false-FAIL again') and NO mechanism to act on it. The only existing breaker is the same-cycle-number dispatcher-deadlock abort (cmd_loop.go:675). Design (all deterministic Go, config-injected thresholds via policy.json, no flags): LAYER 1 — per-task repeat quarantine: at cycle finalize, if the new FailedRecord matches a prior UNRESOLVED FailedRecord on (task id, failed phase, classification) within the retention window, MOVE the driving inbox item to .evolve/inbox/blocked/<id>.json with a reason sidecar (blocked_by: <failure signature>) so triage cannot re-pick it; auto-unblock when the linked fix item ships (recurrence ledger already links pattern->item) or the operator moves it back. One repeat maximum, ever. LAYER 2 — wave-signature breaker: after a 0/N wave where all lanes' FailedRecords share a signature (same failed phase + same classification), the supervisor writes .evolve/loop-blocked.json {signature, cycles, evidence paths} and exits stop_reason=blocked instead of dispatching the next wave (generalizes the existing allFamiliesQuotaExhausted checkpoint from quota to any shared systemic signature). LAYER 3 — pre-dispatch blocking gate: evolve loop boot (and each wave start) refuses to dispatch while loop-blocked.json exists OR any carryover P0 tagged blocking=true is unresolved — prints the blockers + the fix items; `evolve loop --acknowledge-blocked` is the explicit operator override. Classification matters: transient/infra-weather signatures (quota family exhaustion) keep the existing pause-and-auto-resume; only SYSTEMIC signatures (repeat false-FAIL, gate-defect, poisoned-task classes) hard-block. This is the process answer to the skills-drift storm (836-849), the false-FAIL burn (853/854), and every future 'burning tokens for zero progress' shape.
acceptance: L1 RED: a second FAIL of the same (task,phase,classification) quarantines the item (file moved + reason sidecar) and triage's next plan cannot select it; unblock path tested (linked fix ships -> item restored)

L2 RED: a simulated 0/2 wave with matching FailedRecord signatures -> supervisor exits stop_reason=blocked + loop-blocked.json carries signature+evidence; a MIXED-signature 0/2 wave does NOT trip it (one bad apple != systemic)
L3 RED: `evolve loop` with loop-blocked.json present refuses dispatch, exit 2, names the blockers; --acknowledge-blocked proceeds and archives the marker
quota-class signatures keep today's checkpoint+resume behavior byte-identical (regression pinned)
thresholds (repeat window, wave-signature min-width) from policy.json with compiled defaults; no env flags
go test -race on touched packages; both reviewers; docs row in runtime-reference
connects_to: retro-preventive-actions-autofile-inbox (retro output should FEED the blocked registry)
source: operator directive 2026-07-16 after the wave-2 0/2 deep dive; evidence cycles 853/854/855 + retro cycle-854
